violations.text: The presence of rodents are not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout the kitchen area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025

The doors to the outside, located in the cafeteria are, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces were observed not clean: - shelf under prep table (droppings) - prep table drawer (droppings) - shelf under cart (droppings) - prep table drawer (food crumbs) - sharing cooler (spilt milk) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/23/2026
The floors under dishwasher area have an accumulation of droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/23/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (hotel pan, cookie scoops, and ladle scoop) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025

The presence of rodents are not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025

The doors to the outside, located around the cafeteria, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
Inspector observed cases of single service items (portion cups/lids) on the storage rack with droppings. PIC discarded items. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/23/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the bottom shelves of tables have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, droppings and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025

The floors throughout the facility (especially behind/under equipment) have an accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

Door to the outside, located in the cafeteria, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025
Milk cooler condenser coils were observed with a heavy accumulation of dust. New Violation. Correct By: 05/10/2025

The floor next to and behind safe in the kitchen area was observed with an accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. PIC swept and mopped. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2025

Proper signage is lacking in the toilet room used by food employees. A sign or poster reminding food employees to wash their hands is required at all hand sinks used by food employees. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023

Hummus and yogurt were observed held at 44-46 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Items were placed in another refrigeration unit. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration unit outside the kitchen door, used for donations. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 05/03/2023
Cherry tomatoes labeled Wash Before Eating were not washed before being packed into lunch packs. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023
